title: Hotel Reservation Service
summary: Extend a tiny in-memory hotel reservation service through rooms, bookings, cancellation, availability, and guest lookup.

evidence.note: Built from Ramp onsite prep guidance: practical backend workflow, ambiguity, state modeling, and system-design-flavored coding.

rubric.label: Problem framing
rubric.excellent: States the half-open interval rule, invalid range behavior, and deterministic room-selection rule before coding.

rubric.label: Data model
rubric.excellent: Chooses clear room, reservation, room-index, and guest-index state without overbuilding.

rubric.label: MVP sequencing
rubric.excellent: Ships additive behavior incrementally while keeping existing operations working.

rubric.label: Correctness
rubric.excellent: Handles overlaps, back-to-back stays, cancellation, active vs canceled reservations, and guest sorting.

rubric.label: Testing and validation
rubric.excellent: Adds or reasons through tests for invalid ranges, overlap boundaries, cancellation, and creation-order tie breaks.

rubric.label: Debugging and recovery
rubric.excellent: Uses visible failures to isolate one invariant or state-update bug at a time.

rubric.label: Communication
rubric.excellent: Keeps the interviewer oriented around contract, invariants, edge cases, and tradeoffs.

checkpoints.title: Read the code
checkpoints.prompt: What does the starter service already do, and what is intentionally missing?

checkpoints.title: Restate contract
checkpoints.prompt: State the reservation interval rule, invalid date behavior, and room-selection tie break.

checkpoints.title: Model choice
checkpoints.prompt: What state and indexes will you keep so booking, cancellation, availability, and guest lookup stay simple?

checkpoints.title: MVP checkpoint
checkpoints.prompt: What operation works end-to-end now, and what visible failure is next?

checkpoints.title: Risk check
checkpoints.prompt: Which edge case is most likely still broken? How would you prove it?

checkpoints.title: Final explanation
checkpoints.prompt: Explain your invariants, complexity, test strategy, and what you would add in production.
